subject: Public Health
title: Micronutrient Deficiencies in Children
description: A micro-lesson that makes Micronutrient Deficiencies in Children usable.

hook.text: Hidden hunger—lacking vitamins and minerals despite eating enough calories—affects 2 billion people worldwide.

buildup.text: Iron deficiency causes anemia in 40% of children under five—impairing brain development and immune function.

discovery.text: Vitamin A supplementation—given twice a year—reduces child mortality by 24% in deficient populations.

twist.text: Zinc supplementation during diarrhea reduces episode duration by 25%—yet most children never receive it.

climax.text: Food fortification, supplementation, and dietary diversity together can eliminate most micronutrient deficiencies.

punchline.text: A child can eat enough food and still starve for the nutrients that matter.

quiz.question: By how much does vitamin A supplementation reduce child mortality?
